articles.title: Family Fun Gets Too Expensive
articles.summary: Bianca and Paul Osborne work hard and want to have fun days out with their two daughters, Amelia and Sienna. But they are cutting back because things like meals and activities cost too much money. For example, a lunch for their family of four at Costa costs over £50, which they think is a lot for just some food. They also went to an aquarium and Laser Quest, adding up to over £120 for the afternoon. They say they can't do this often because after paying bills, there isn't much money left. Other families feel the same way, and places like Costa and Laser Quest say they have high costs too. This shows how rising prices make it hard for middle-income families to enjoy special outings.

articles.title: Earth's Climate in Danger: UN Warns of Too Much Heat
articles.summary: The United Nations says Earth's climate is in big trouble because it's getting too hot. This is caused by gases like carbon dioxide from burning coal and oil, which trap heat. Last year, the ocean got very warm and ice caps melted more. Scientists worry that a natural warm weather pattern called El Niño might make things even hotter soon. The UN leader, António Guterres, wants countries to stop using fossil fuels and switch to clean energy like solar and wind to keep everyone safe. He says every sign of climate change is warning us. The last 11 years have been the hottest ever recorded, and 2025 was 1.43 degrees Celsius warmer than before people used lots of fossil fuels. Even though a cool pattern called La Niña made 2025 a bit cooler, it was still one of the three hottest years. The Earth is taking in more heat energy than it can release, which is making the climate change fast.

articles.title: Heat Pumps: A New Way to Heat Homes
articles.summary: This article talks about heat pumps, which are machines that heat homes using electricity instead of burning gas or oil. The author has a heat pump in her old house in France and likes it because it works well after being fixed. She thinks heat pumps are exciting because they could change how people stay warm, like how cars replaced horses. In the UK, the government wants people to use heat pumps to help the environment, but they cost a lot of money to buy and run. A family in London installed a heat pump and spent £17,000, but they got a £5,000 grant from the government. They also improved their home's insulation, which helped save energy. After installing the heat pump, they used much less energy, cutting it by almost two-thirds. Heat pumps are very efficient and used in many homes in Europe, but they are still expensive for most people.

articles.title: New Towns Plan: Seven Places for New Homes
articles.summary: The government in England has picked seven areas where they might build new towns. These are places like Tempsford, Leeds South Bank, and Manchester Victoria North. They want to make new homes for people. The plan was announced by the Labour party in 2023. They promise to start building in three of these areas before the next election. Some other places were not chosen for now, but they could still get help later. The new towns will be built near big cities or as expansions of existing towns. This is like what happened after World War Two when towns like Stevenage were built. The government will talk to people about these plans before making final decisions.

articles.title: Chileans Protest for Water and Nature
articles.summary: In Chile, many people marched in cities like Santiago on World Water Day. They were upset because the new president, José Antonio Kast, stopped 43 rules that protected the environment. These rules helped animals like frogs and penguins, kept water clean, and reduced pollution from factories. The protesters say Kast is hurting nature to help big companies make money. They want everyone to have clean drinking water, but 1.4 million people in Chile still don't have it. Kast says he cares about jobs and the environment, but the marchers think he is making things worse for nature. This protest shows how people can come together to stand up for what they believe in.

articles.title: President Trump's Changing Plans for a Waterway
articles.summary: President Donald Trump is dealing with a problem in the Strait of Hormuz, an important waterway for oil ships. He keeps changing his ideas on how to fix it. First, he wanted to use diplomacy and talk with other countries. Then, he lifted some sanctions on Iran to help with high gas prices. Now, he is threatening to attack Iran's power plants if they don't open the strait in 48 hours. Critics say he doesn't have a clear plan and is panicking. The situation is urgent because oil prices are going up, affecting people around the world. Trump's actions have caused confusion and worry about whether the U.S. is ready for war.
